effort_complexity.rationale: Two files, one function each, same fix pattern.

design_decisions.choice: New Oracle.ReadReleaseWorktreePath + OracleReaderAdapter.ReadReleaseWorktreePath method in internal/board/oracle.go, outside spec.json's literal touchpoint list (which names only the two cmd/sworn files and their tests).
design_decisions.rationale: The board.json/index.md dual-path resolution logic already lives in oracle.go's readTrackInfos; duplicating it inside cmd/sworn/merge.go would recreate the exact drifting-second-copy bug class this release exists to close. Narrow, purely additive, no existing caller touched.

design_decisions.choice: regress.go resolves via the disk-based board.ReadBoard (repoRoot + release), merge.go resolves via the git-ref-based Oracle.ReadReleaseWorktreePath through the OracleReaderAdapter it already builds for gate 1 — two different resolution mechanisms for the same field, not unified onto one.
design_decisions.rationale: regress.go has no git-ref concept anywhere else in the file (a plain local-directory tool); merge.go already resolves everything via git refs (release-wt worktree may be on a different branch than the launch directory). Matches the precedented S04 split (git-ref oracle where a repo already exists, board.ReadBoard where it doesn't).

design_decisions.choice: Delete resolveReleaseWorktree/extractFrontmatterBody (merge.go) and extractReleaseWorktreePath (regress.go) outright rather than deprecate.
design_decisions.rationale: No other callers (confirmed by grep) once both call sites switch to the oracle/board.ReadBoard. Dead frontmatter-parsing code left in place is the same attractive-nuisance class S04's design.md flagged and removed.
